_README: WHAT THIS IS — file purposes for the project registry (.aspis/index/FILE_REGISTRY.yaml).
It tells the system what a file *is*, for files whose purpose can't be read from their
own first line or docstring (data files, configs, assets). The registry script runs with
bare Python (no pyyaml), so this file stays JSON. Keys starting with '_' are help and
examples only — the system ignores them; edit the 'files' / 'names' / 'patterns' maps below.

_layers.files: EXACT path -> purpose. Highest priority. Use for one specific file in this repo.
_layers.names: FILENAME (basename) -> purpose. Applies to every file with that name, anywhere.
_layers.patterns: GLOB -> purpose. Matched against the basename, then the path; first match wins.

_how_to_use: 1. List files that have no purpose yet:  python .aspis/scripts/context/build_registry.py --check
2. Add an entry in the most specific map that fits (files > names > patterns).
3. Re-run the registry (any 'aspis' context update does this) to apply.
Priority when more than one matches: files, then names, then patterns.

files.src/app/main.py: FastAPI entrypoint — wires routers and startup
